Petitioner Liliana Valenzuela'S Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony
Off Calendar Mar-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo J. Gonzalez for Petitioner and moving party Liliana Valenzuela
Douglas M. Press for respondent and opposing party Jack O'connell

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel answers at deposition is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to the questions that ask the witnesses to describe what had been done before their arrival to study alternatives to the exit exam. The questions are of a factual nature and seek information as to what activities were undertaken to comply with the requirements of Education Code Section 60856. The motion is denied as to the remaining questions. The Court has balanced the need for disclosure and the availability of the information through other sources against the interest in maintaining the confidentiality of discussions leading up to the final decision as to providing alternatives. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Hearing On Expedited Discovery
Off Calendar Feb-17-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo J. Gonzales for petitioner and moving party: Liliana Valenzuela
Douglas M. Press for respondent and opposing party: State of California and Jack O'Connell, Public Schools Superintendent
After Hearing Petitioner's Motion for expedited discovery is GRANTED. Written discovery due March 8, 2006. Deposition may commence after March 13, 2006. Prevailing party to prepare order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
